NKJV.1: My son, if you receive my words, And treasure my commands within you,
NKJV.2: So that you incline your ear to wisdom, And apply your heart to understanding;
NKJV.3: Yes, if you cry out for discernment, And lift up your voice for understanding,
NKJV.4: If you seek her as silver, And search for her as for hidden treasures;
NKJV.5: Then you will understand the fear of the LORD, And find the knowledge of God.
NKJV.6: For the LORD gives wisdom; From His mouth come knowledge and understanding;
NKJV.7: He stores up sound wisdom for the upright; He is a shield to those who walk uprightly;
NKJV.8: He guards the paths of justice, And preserves the way of His saints.
NKJV.9: Then you will understand righteousness and justice, Equity and every good path.
NKJV.10: When wisdom enters your heart, And knowledge is pleasant to your soul,
NKJV.11: Discretion will preserve you;  Understanding will keep you,
NKJV.12: To deliver you from the way of evil, From the man who speaks perverse things,
NKJV.13: From those who leave the paths of uprightness To walk in the ways of darkness;
NKJV.14: Who rejoice in doing evil, And delight in the perversity of the wicked;
NKJV.15: Whose ways are crooked, And who are devious in their paths;
NKJV.16: To deliver you from the immoral woman, From the seductress who flatters with her words,
NKJV.17: Who forsakes the companion of her youth, And forgets the covenant of her God.
NKJV.18: For her house leads down to death, And her paths to the dead;
NKJV.19: None who go to her return, Nor do they regain the paths of life—
NKJV.20: So you may walk in the way of goodness, And keep to the paths of righteousness.
NKJV.21: For the upright will dwell in the land, And the blameless will remain in it;
NKJV.22: But the wicked will be cut off from the earth, And the unfaithful will be uprooted from it.

KJV.1: My son<S>1121</S>, if thou wilt receive<S>3947</S> my words<S>561</S>, and hide<S>6845</S> my commandments<S>4687</S> with thee;
KJV.2: So that thou incline<S>7181</S> thine ear<S>241</S> unto wisdom<S>2451</S>, and apply<S>5186</S> thine heart<S>3820</S> to understanding<S>8394</S>;

KJV.6: For the LORD<S>3068</S> giveth<S>5414</S> wisdom<S>2451</S>: out of his mouth<S>6310</S> cometh knowledge<S>1847</S> and understanding<S>8394</S>.
KJV.7: He layeth up<S>6845</S> <S>6845</S> sound wisdom<S>8454</S> for the righteous<S>3477</S>: he is a buckler<S>4043</S> to them that walk<S>1980</S> uprightly<S>8537</S>.
KJV.8: He keepeth<S>5341</S> the paths<S>734</S> of judgment<S>4941</S>, and preserveth<S>8104</S> the way<S>1870</S> of his saints<S>2623</S>.
KJV.9: Then shalt thou understand<S>995</S> righteousness<S>6664</S>, and judgment<S>4941</S>, and equity<S>4339</S>; yea, every good<S>2896</S> path<S>4570</S>.
KJV.10: When wisdom<S>2451</S> entereth<S>935</S> into thine heart<S>3820</S>, and knowledge<S>1847</S> is pleasant<S>5276</S> unto thy soul<S>5315</S>;
KJV.11: Discretion<S>4209</S> shall preserve<S>8104</S> thee, understanding<S>8394</S> shall keep<S>5341</S> thee:
KJV.12: To deliver<S>5337</S> thee from the way<S>1870</S> of the evil<S>7451</S> man, from the man<S>376</S> that speaketh<S>1696</S> froward things<S>8419</S>;
KJV.13: Who leave<S>5800</S> the paths<S>734</S> of uprightness<S>3476</S>, to walk<S>3212</S> in the ways<S>1870</S> of darkness<S>2822</S>;
KJV.14: Who rejoice<S>8056</S> to do<S>6213</S> evil<S>7451</S>, and delight<S>1523</S> in the frowardness<S>8419</S> of the wicked<S>7451</S>;
KJV.15: Whose ways<S>734</S> are crooked<S>6141</S>, and they froward<S>3868</S> in their paths<S>4570</S>:
KJV.16: To deliver<S>5337</S> thee from the strange<S>2114</S> woman<S>802</S>, even from the stranger<S>5237</S> which flattereth<S>2505</S> with her words<S>561</S>;
KJV.17: Which forsaketh<S>5800</S> the guide<S>441</S> of her youth<S>5271</S>, and forgetteth<S>7911</S> the covenant<S>1285</S> of her God<S>430</S>.
KJV.18: For her house<S>1004</S> inclineth<S>7743</S> unto death<S>4194</S>, and her paths<S>4570</S> unto the dead<S>7496</S>.
KJV.19: None that go<S>935</S> unto her return again<S>7725</S>, neither take they hold<S>5381</S> of the paths<S>734</S> of life<S>2416</S>.
KJV.20: That thou mayest walk<S>3212</S> in the way<S>1870</S> of good<S>2896</S> men, and keep<S>8104</S> the paths<S>734</S> of the righteous<S>6662</S>.
KJV.21: For the upright<S>3477</S> shall dwell<S>7931</S> in the land<S>776</S>, and the perfect<S>8549</S> shall remain<S>3498</S> in it.

ESV.1: My son, if you receive my words and treasure up my commandments with you,
ESV.2: making your ear attentive to wisdom and inclining your heart to understanding;
ESV.3: yes, if you call out for insight and raise your voice for understanding,
ESV.4: if you seek it like silver and search for it as for hidden treasures,
ESV.5: then you will understand the fear of the LORD and find the knowledge of God.
ESV.6: For the LORD gives wisdom; from his mouth come knowledge and understanding;
ESV.7: he stores up sound wisdom for the upright; he is a shield to those who walk in integrity,
ESV.8: guarding the paths of justice and watching over the way of his saints.
ESV.9: Then you will understand righteousness and justice and equity, every good path;
ESV.10: for wisdom will come into your heart, and knowledge will be pleasant to your soul;
ESV.11: discretion will watch over you, understanding will guard you,
ESV.12: delivering you from the way of evil, from men of perverted speech,
ESV.13: who forsake the paths of uprightness to walk in the ways of darkness,
ESV.14: who rejoice in doing evil and delight in the perverseness of evil,
ESV.15: men whose paths are crooked, and who are devious in their ways.
ESV.16: So you will be delivered from the forbidden  woman, from the adulteress  with her smooth words,
ESV.17: who forsakes the companion of her youth and forgets the covenant of her God;
ESV.18: for her house sinks down to death, and her paths to the departed;
ESV.19: none who go to her come back, nor do they regain the paths of life.
ESV.20: So you will walk in the way of the good and keep to the paths of the righteous.
ESV.21: For the upright will inhabit the land, and those with integrity will remain in it,
ESV.22: but the wicked will be cut off from the land, and the treacherous will be rooted out of it.

NASB.1: My son, if you will receive my words And treasure my commandments within you,
NASB.2: Make your ear attentive to wisdom, Incline your heart to understanding;
NASB.3: For if you cry for discernment, Lift your voice for understanding;
NASB.4: If you seek her as silver And search for her as for hidden treasures;
NASB.5: Then you will discern the fear of the LORD And discover the knowledge of God.
NASB.6: For the LORD gives wisdom; From His mouth [come] knowledge and understanding.
NASB.7: He stores up sound wisdom for the upright; [He is] a shield to those who walk in integrity,
NASB.8: Guarding the paths of justice, And He preserves the way of His godly ones.
NASB.9: Then you will discern righteousness and justice And equity [and] every good course.
NASB.10: For wisdom will enter your heart And knowledge will be pleasant to your soul;
NASB.11: Discretion will guard you, Understanding will watch over you,
NASB.12: To deliver you from the way of evil, From the man who speaks perverse things;
NASB.13: From those who leave the paths of uprightness To walk in the ways of darkness;
NASB.14: Who delight in doing evil And rejoice in the perversity of evil;
NASB.15: Whose paths are crooked, And who are devious in their ways;
NASB.16: To deliver you from the strange woman, From the adulteress who flatters with her words;
NASB.17: That leaves the companion of her youth And forgets the covenant of her God;
NASB.18: For her house sinks down to death And her tracks [lead] to the dead;
NASB.19: None who go to her return again, Nor do they reach the paths of life.
NASB.20: So you will walk in the way of good men And keep to the paths of the righteous.
NASB.21: For the upright will live in the land And the blameless will remain in it;
NASB.22: But the wicked will be cut off from the land And the treacherous will be uprooted from it.

NLT.1:
My child, listen to what I say,
and treasure my commands.
NLT.2:
Tune your ears to wisdom,
and concentrate on understanding.
NLT.3:
Cry out for insight,
and ask for understanding.
NLT.4:
Search for them as you would for silver;
seek them like hidden treasures.
NLT.5:
Then you will understand what it means to fear the LORD,
and you will gain knowledge of God.
NLT.6:
For the LORD grants wisdom!
From his mouth come knowledge and understanding.
NLT.7:
He grants a treasure of common sense to the honest.
He is a shield to those who walk with integrity.
NLT.8:
He guards the paths of the just
and protects those who are faithful to him.
NLT.9:
Then you will understand what is right, just, and fair,
and you will find the right way to go.
NLT.10:
For wisdom will enter your heart,
and knowledge will fill you with joy.
NLT.11:
Wise choices will watch over you.
Understanding will keep you safe.
NLT.12:
Wisdom will save you from evil people,
from those whose words are twisted.
NLT.13:
These men turn from the right way
to walk down dark paths.
NLT.14:
They take pleasure in doing wrong,
and they enjoy the twisted ways of evil.
NLT.15:
Their actions are crooked,
and their ways are wrong.
NLT.16:
Wisdom will save you from the immoral woman,
from the seductive words of the promiscuous woman.
NLT.17:
She has abandoned her husband
and ignores the covenant she made before God.
NLT.18:
Entering her house leads to death;
it is the road to the grave.
NLT.19:
The man who visits her is doomed.
He will never reach the paths of life.
NLT.20:
So follow the steps of the good,
and stay on the paths of the righteous.
NLT.21:
For only the godly will live in the land,
and those with integrity will remain in it.
NLT.22:
But the wicked will be removed from the land,
and the treacherous will be uprooted.

WEB.1: My son, if you will receive my words, and store up my commandments within you;
WEB.2: So as to turn your ear to wisdom, and apply your heart to understanding;
WEB.3: Yes, if you call out for discernment, and lift up your voice for understanding;
WEB.4: If you seek her as silver, and search for her as for hidden treasures:
WEB.5: then you will understand the fear of Yahweh, and find the knowledge of God.
WEB.6: For Yahweh gives wisdom. Out of his mouth comes knowledge and understanding.
WEB.7: He lays up sound wisdom for the upright. He is a shield to those who walk in integrity;
WEB.8: that he may guard the paths of justice, and preserve the way of his saints.
WEB.9: Then you will understand righteousness and justice, equity and every good path.
WEB.10: For wisdom will enter into your heart. Knowledge will be pleasant to your soul.
WEB.11: Discretion will watch over you. Understanding will keep you,
WEB.12: to deliver you from the way of evil, from the men who speak perverse things;
WEB.13: who forsake the paths of uprightness, to walk in the ways of darkness;
WEB.14: who rejoice to do evil, and delight in the perverseness of evil;
WEB.15: who are crooked in their ways, and wayward in their paths:
WEB.16: To deliver you from the strange woman, even from the foreigner who flatters with her words;
WEB.17: who forsakes the friend of her youth, and forgets the covenant of her God:
WEB.18: for her house leads down to death, her paths to the departed spirits.
WEB.19: None who go to her return again, neither do they attain to the paths of life:
WEB.20: that you may walk in the way of good men, and keep the paths of the righteous.
WEB.21: For the upright will dwell in the land. The perfect will remain in it.
WEB.22: But the wicked will be cut off from the land. The treacherous will be rooted out of it.

AMP.1:
My son, if you will receive my words
And treasure my commandments within you,
AMP.2:
So that your ear is attentive to [skillful and godly] wisdom,
And apply your heart to understanding [seeking it conscientiously and striving for it eagerly];
AMP.3:
Yes, if you cry out for insight,
And lift up your voice for understanding;
AMP.4:
If you seek skillful and godly wisdom as you would silver
And search for her as you would hidden treasures;
AMP.5:
Then you will understand the [reverent] fear of the LORD [that is, worshiping Him and regarding Him as truly awesome]
And discover the knowledge of God.
AMP.6:
For the LORD gives [skillful and godly] wisdom;
From His mouth come knowledge and understanding.
AMP.7:
He stores away sound wisdom for the righteous [those who are in right standing with Him];
He is a shield to those who walk in integrity [those of honorable character and moral courage],
AMP.8:
He guards the paths of justice;
And He preserves the way of His saints (believers).
AMP.9:
Then you will understand righteousness and justice [in every circumstance]
And integrity and every good path.
AMP.10:
For [skillful and godly] wisdom will enter your heart
And knowledge will be pleasant to your soul.
AMP.11:
Discretion will watch over you,
Understanding and discernment will guard you,
AMP.12:
To keep you from the way of evil and the evil man,
From the man who speaks perverse things;
AMP.13:
From those who leave the paths of uprightness
To walk in the ways of darkness;
AMP.14:
Who find joy in doing evil
And delight in the perversity of evil,
AMP.15:
Whose paths are crooked,
And who are devious in their ways;
AMP.16:
To keep you from the immoral woman;
From the seductress with her flattering words,
AMP.17:
Who leaves the companion (husband) of her youth,
And forgets the covenant of her God.
AMP.18:
For her house leads down to death
And her paths lead to the dead;
AMP.19:
None who go to her return again,
Nor do they regain the paths of life—
AMP.20:
So you will walk in the way of good men [that is, those of personal integrity, moral courage and honorable character],
And keep to the paths of the righteous.
AMP.21:
For the upright [those who are in right standing with God] will live in the land
And those [of integrity] who are blameless [in God's sight] will remain in it;
AMP.22:
But the wicked will be cut off from the land
And the treacherous shall be [forcibly] uprooted and removed from it.

MSG.1:
Good friend, take to heart what I'm telling you;
collect my counsels and guard them with your life.
MSG.2:
Tune your ears to the world of Wisdom;
set your heart on a life of Understanding.
MSG.3:
That's right—if you make Insight your priority,
and won't take no for an answer,
MSG.4:
Searching for it like a prospector panning for gold,
like an adventurer on a treasure hunt,
MSG.5:
Believe me, before you know it Fear-of-God will be yours;
you'll have come upon the Knowledge of God.
MSG.6:
And here's why: God gives out Wisdom free,
is plainspoken in Knowledge and Understanding.
MSG.7:
He's a rich mine of Common Sense for those who live well,
a personal bodyguard to the candid and sincere.
MSG.8:
He keeps his eye on all who live honestly,
and pays special attention to his loyally committed ones.
MSG.9:
So now you can pick out what's true and fair,
find all the good trails!
MSG.10:
Lady Wisdom will be your close friend,
and Brother Knowledge your pleasant companion.
MSG.11:
Good Sense will scout ahead for danger,
Insight will keep an eye out for you.
MSG.12:
They'll keep you from making wrong turns,
or following the bad directions
MSG.13:
Of those who are lost themselves
and can't tell a trail from a tumbleweed,
MSG.14:
These losers who make a game of evil
and throw parties to celebrate perversity,
MSG.15:
Traveling paths that go nowhere,
wandering in a maze of detours and dead ends.
MSG.16:
Wise friends will rescue you from the Temptress—
that smooth-talking Seductress
MSG.17:
Who's faithless to the husband she married years ago,
never gave a second thought to her promises before God.
MSG.18:
Her whole way of life is doomed;
every step she takes brings her closer to hell.
MSG.19:
No one who joins her company ever comes back,
ever sets foot on the path to real living.
MSG.20:
So—join the company of good men and women,
keep your feet on the tried and true paths.
MSG.21:
It's the men who walk straight who will settle this land,
the women with integrity who will last here.
MSG.22:
The corrupt will lose their lives;
the dishonest will be gone for good.

NIV.1:
Moral Benefits of Wisdom
My son, if you accept my words
and store up my commands within you,
NIV.2:
turning your ear to wisdom
and applying your heart to understanding,
NIV.3:
and if you call out for insight
and cry aloud for understanding,
NIV.4:
and if you look for it as for silver
and search for it as for hidden treasure,
NIV.5:
then you will understand the fear of the Lord
and find the knowledge of God.
NIV.6:
For the Lord gives wisdom,
and from his mouth come knowledge and understanding.
NIV.7:
He holds victory in store for the upright,
he is a shield to those whose walk is blameless,
NIV.8:
for he guards the course of the just
and protects the way of his faithful ones.
NIV.9:
Then you will understand what is right and just
and fair—every good path.
NIV.10:
For wisdom will enter your heart,
and knowledge will be pleasant to your soul.
NIV.11:
Discretion will protect you,
and understanding will guard you.
NIV.12:
Wisdom will save you from the ways of wicked men,
from men whose words are perverse,
NIV.13:
who leave the straight paths
to walk in dark ways,
NIV.14:
who delight in doing wrong
and rejoice in the perverseness of evil,
NIV.15:
whose paths are crooked
and who are devious in their ways.
NIV.16:
It will save you also from the adulteress,
from the wayward wife with her seductive words,
NIV.17:
who has left the partner of her youth
and ignored the covenant she made before God.
NIV.18:
For her house leads down to death
and her paths to the spirits of the dead.
NIV.19:
None who go to her return
or attain the paths of life.
NIV.20:
Thus you will walk in the ways of good men
and keep to the paths of the righteous.
NIV.21:
For the upright will live in the land,
and the blameless will remain in it;
NIV.22:
but the wicked will be cut off from the land,
and the unfaithful will be torn from it.

NRSVCE.1:
My child, if you accept my words
and treasure up my commandments within you,
NRSVCE.2:
making your ear attentive to wisdom
and inclining your heart to understanding;
NRSVCE.3:
if you indeed cry out for insight,
and raise your voice for understanding;
NRSVCE.4:
if you seek it like silver,
and search for it as for hidden treasures —
NRSVCE.5:
then you will understand the fear of the LORD
and find the knowledge of God.
NRSVCE.6:
For the LORD gives wisdom;
from his mouth come knowledge and understanding;
NRSVCE.7:
he stores up sound wisdom for the upright;
he is a shield to those who walk blamelessly,
NRSVCE.8:
guarding the paths of justice
and preserving the way of his faithful ones.
NRSVCE.9:
Then you will understand righteousness and justice
and equity, every good path;
NRSVCE.10:
for wisdom will come into your heart,
and knowledge will be pleasant to your soul;
NRSVCE.11:
prudence will watch over you;
and understanding will guard you.
NRSVCE.12:
It will save you from the way of evil,
from those who speak perversely,
NRSVCE.13:
who forsake the paths of uprightness
to walk in the ways of darkness,
NRSVCE.14:
who rejoice in doing evil
and delight in the perverseness of evil;
NRSVCE.15:
those whose paths are crooked,
and who are devious in their ways.
NRSVCE.16:
You will be saved from the loose woman,
from the adulteress with her smooth words,
NRSVCE.17:
who forsakes the partner of her youth
and forgets her sacred covenant;
NRSVCE.18:
for her way leads down to death,
and her paths to the shades;
NRSVCE.19:
those who go to her never come back,
nor do they regain the paths of life.
NRSVCE.20:
Therefore walk in the way of the good,
and keep to the paths of the just.
NRSVCE.21:
For the upright will abide in the land,
and the innocent will remain in it;
NRSVCE.22:
but the wicked will be cut off from the land,
and the treacherous will be rooted out of it.

NET.1: My child, if you receive my words, and store up my commands within you,
NET.2: by making your ear attentive to wisdom, and by turning your heart to understanding,
NET.3: indeed, if you call out for discernment — raise your voice for understanding —
NET.4: if you seek it like silver, and search for it like hidden treasure,
NET.5: then you will understand how to fear the LORD, and you will discover knowledge about God.
NET.6: For the LORD gives wisdom, and from his mouth comes knowledge and understanding.
NET.7: He stores up effective counsel for the upright, and is like a shield for those who live with integrity,
NET.8: to guard the paths of the righteous and to protect the way of his pious ones.
NET.9: Then you will understand righteousness and justice and equity — every good way.
NET.10: For wisdom will enter your heart, and moral knowledge will be attractive to you.
NET.11: Discretion will protect you, understanding will guard you,
NET.12: to deliver you from the way of the wicked, from those speaking perversity,
NET.13: who leave the upright paths to walk on the dark ways,
NET.14: who delight in doing evil, they rejoice in perverse evil;
NET.15: whose paths are morally crooked, and who are devious in their ways;
NET.16: to deliver you from the adulteress, from the sexually loose woman who speaks flattering words;
NET.17: who leaves the husband from her younger days, and forgets her marriage covenant made before God.
NET.18: For her house sinks down to death, and her paths lead to the place of the departed spirits.
NET.19: None who go in to her will return, nor will they reach the paths of life.
NET.20: So you will walk in the way of good people, and will keep on the paths of the righteous.
NET.21: For the upright will reside in the land, and those with integrity will remain in it,
NET.22: but the wicked will be removed from the land, and the treacherous will be torn away from it.
